data.title: Unusual Machines secures $5M+ deal in counter-UAS; deliveries start in April
data.description:
• Unusual Machines (UMAC) announced on Tuesday that it has received an order worth $5M+ from Powerus, an Autonomous Power Corporation.
  • The company will supply core components for counter-UAS systems and 10-inch drones, showing rising demand for such tech.
  • The deliveries will start in April and are expected to be completed by Q2.
  • Powerus focuses on modern drone systems, including anti-drone tech, and is also planning a merger with Aureus Greenway Holdings (AGH).
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump keeps pressure on Powell as Warsh faces lawmakers
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump signaled Tuesday that he is not backing away from a Justice Department investigation tied to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, maintaining pressure on the central bank as Kevin Warsh prepares to appear before Congress.

Speaking to CNBC, Trump said officials need to “find out” why the Fed spent so much on renovating its headquarters. The president brushed aside suggestions that he might drop the probe to clear the way for Warsh, even joking that his nominee may need an office at the White House because the Fed building “is not going to be done.”

The remarks are expected to sharpen scrutiny during Warsh’s appearance before lawmakers at 10 a.m. ET, where questions about Fed independence will likely dominate the hearing.

Trump also made clear he expects rate cuts from a future Fed chair. Asked whether he would be disappointed if Warsh did not lower rates, the president said he would, reinforcing political pressure already surrounding the central bank.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Rio Tinto racks up higher Q1 iron ore, copper production
data.description:
Rio Tinto (RIO) said Tuesday it produced more iron ore, copper, and aluminum during Q1, while seeking to reassure investors of limited impacts so far from the Middle East ​war on its supply chains in this year's H2.

The company said it has relatively limited visibility of how the conflict will affect supply chains in this year's H2, but that it has contingency plans in place.

The "mix and scale of our portfolio has ensured growth and supply chain resilience against changing operating conditions as we continue to closely monitor the evolving situation in the Middle East," CEO Simon Trott said.

The world's second-biggest miner by market value reported a 13% Y/Y increase in production from its Pilbara iron ore operations to 78.8M tons, the company's second-highest for the period since 2018, "driven by continued investment in mine health and productivity and fewer weather impacts."

Shipments from the Pilbara operations were hurt by two tropical cyclones during the quarter, reducing shipments by ~8M tons, although roughly half of the weather-related losses are expected to be recovered, Rio (RIO) said; total Q1 sales rose 2% Y/Y to 72.4M tons.

Q1 copper production rose 9% Y/Y to 229K tons, underpinned by a 56% jump in output at the Oyu Tolgoi mine in Mongolia, where Rio (RIO) is ramping up production from an underground expansion.

Rio (RIO), which reported a 1% increase in Q1 aluminum output to 848K tons, said it has "ensured security of supply to our customers."

The result was a strong start to the year across Rio Tinto's (RIO) businesses, Morgan Stanley analyst Rahul Anand said in a note.

data.title: ETFs extend their historic inflow streak to 82 months
data.description:
Exchange-traded funds continue to attract steady investor demand, underscoring their growing role in modern portfolios. In a notable milestone, ETFs have recorded 82 consecutive months of positive net inflows, marking nearly seven years of uninterrupted capital growth.

The streak reflects consistent interest from both institutional and retail investors, drawn by ETFs’ liquidity, transparency, and cost efficiency. This sustained momentum highlights the structural shift toward passive and diversified investment strategies across global markets. 

Outlined below are Wall Street’s top 20 largest ETFs from an assets under management perspective: 

  1. Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), $895.30B in AUM. 
  2. iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV), $774.29B in AUM. 
  3. State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY), $714.80B in AUM. 
  4. Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI), $607.33B in AUM.
  5. Invesco QQQ Trust Series I (QQQ), $420.54B in AUM. 
  6. Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF (VEA), $219.91B in AUM. 
  7. Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG), $207.43B in AUM.
  8.  iShares Core MSCI EAFE ETF (IEFA), $180.88B in AUM.
  9. Vanguard Value ETF (VTV), $169.32B in AUM. 
  10. SPDR Gold Shares (GLD), $163.43B in AUM.
  11. Vanguard Total Bond Market ETF (BND), $152.61B in AUM. 
  12. iShares Core MSCI Emerging Markets ETF (IEMG), $148.91B in AUM. 
  13. Vanguard Total International Stock ETF (VXUS), $144.03B in AUM. 
  14. iShares Core U.S. Aggregate Bond ETF (AGG), $135.87B in AUM. 
  15. State Street SPDR Portfolio S&P 500 ETF (SPYM), $133.91B in AUM. 
  16. iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF), $122.76B in AUM. 
  17. Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT), $119.19B in AUM. 
  18. Vanguard FTSE Emerging Markets ETF (VWO), $118.40B in AUM. 
  19. iShares Core S&P Mid-Cap ETF (IJH), $113.23B in AUM. 
  20. Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF (VIG), $104.04B in AUM.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Tuesday: MMM, RTX, NOC, TSCO
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Tuesday as renewed hopes for a peace deal between Iran and the U.S. lifted sentiment. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

3M (MMM) slipped 1.8% in premarket trading Tuesday after reporting first-quarter results that matched Wall Street revenue expectations and topped profit forecasts. The industrial conglomerate posted revenue of $6.0 billion, in line with analysts’ consensus estimate, while adjusted earnings came in at $2.14 a share, ahead of expectations for $1.98. Investors weighed a cautious macro backdrop against improving margins and reaffirmed full-year guidance.

Northrop Grumman (NOC) fell about 1% in premarket trading Tuesday despite beating Wall Street estimates for the first quarter. The defense contractor reported first-quarter revenue of $9.88 billion, above analysts’ consensus estimate of $9.76 billion, with earnings of $6.14 a share also topping expectations of $6.06. Shares declined as investors focused on full-year guidance that largely matched revenue expectations while trailing profit forecasts.

RTX (RTX) gained 2.4% in premarket trading Tuesday after beating Wall Street expectations for the first quarter and lifting portions of its full-year outlook. The aerospace and defense company reported first-quarter revenue of $22.1 billion, ahead of analysts’ consensus estimate of $21.47 billion. Investors cheered strong defense demand and continued aerospace recovery.

Tractor Supply (TSCO) dropped 5.45% in premarket trading Tuesday after falling short of expectations with its first-quarter earnings report. Sales increased 3.6% during the quarter to $3.59 billion, driven by robust new store openings and, to a lesser extent, growth in comparable store sales.

data.title: JFB Construction flags XTEND wins $1.67M Israel defense deal
data.description:
• JFB Construction (JFB) on Tuesday announced that XTEND (XTND) won a $1.67M contract from Israel’s Defense Ministry to supply drone systems.
  • The delivery of these drones is expected to happen in 2026.
  • XTEND (XTND) is already working with defense clients in 30 countries and expects more (and bigger) deals in 2026.
  • This deal strengthens the company’s position in AI-based defense robotics, though exact details are not shared.
  • Moreover, JFB Construction (JFB) and XTEND (XTND) are set to merge in an all-stock deal, and after the deal, the combined company will be listed in the U.S. as XTEND AI Robotics (XTND).
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eastern Bankshares seen extending dividend growth streak this April
data.description:
Eastern Bankshares (EBC) is anticipated to announce a dividend this April, alongside its first quarter results.

Based on historical trends and Eastern Bankshares’ (EBC) dividend profile, including a 32.69% payout ratio, a 54.02% five-year dividend growth rate, and four consecutive years of dividend increases, analysts expect a consensus annual dividend of $0.54 per share.

That would imply a quarterly payout of $0.135, or a 3.8% increase from the current quarterly distribution of $0.13. The company last raised its dividend last April by 8.3% to $0.13 from a prior dividend of $0.12.

 

 

Eastern Bankshares (EBC) is scheduled to report Q1 results on April 23, 2026.

data.title: NETSTREIT declares $0.22 dividend
data.description:
• NETSTREIT (NTST) declares $0.22/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.21%
  • Payable June 15; for shareholders of record June 1; ex-div June 1.
  • See NTST Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Newmark upsizes credit facility to $900M
data.description:
• Newmark Group (NMRK) said on Tuesday it had revised its senior unsecured revolving credit facility, increasing its size by 50% to $900M and extending the maturity to April 17, 2030.
  • The company had the option to expand the facility up to $1.1B.
  • Borrowings under the credit facility will bear interest, at the company’s option, based on Term SOFR or a base rate, in each case plus an applicable margin of 1.625% or 0.625%, respectively, subject to the company’s credit rating.
  • The facility replaces a $600 million revolving credit facility maturing April 26, 2027, and implies an indicative Term SOFR rate of about 5.27% as of April 17, 2026.
  • The company expects to use its credit facility for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Kratos completes initial flights of upgraded Firejet drone with J85 engine
data.description:
• Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS) on Tuesday said it has completed initial flight tests of a new version of its Firejet unmanned aerial system equipped with its J85 engine, marking a second major configuration of the platform.
  • The upgraded “Mk1 Firejet” is designed to deliver improved range, endurance, speed and climb rate, while allowing customers to choose between the new model and the existing “Classic Firejet” depending on operational needs.
  • The Classic Firejet, in service since the early 2010s using JetCat engines, is widely used by the U.S. Army’s Target Systems Management Office and allied countries, including Taiwan.
  • Kratos said production of its Spartan engine line, including the J85, is ramping up following the opening of a new facility in late 2025, with output expected to reach thousands of units this year.
  • The company added that the new Firejet configuration can serve both as a target system and as a tactical unmanned aircraft, positioned in the sub-$500,000 category, with capabilities including high subsonic speed, maneuverability, and modular mission systems.
  • KTOS +1.56% premarket to $70.9217.
  • Source: Press Release
